tools.name: lsp_goto_definition
tools.description: Find where a function, class, or variable is defined. More accurate than grep—handles aliases, re-exports, and overloads correctly. Returns file path, line, and column of the definition.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_goto_type_definition
tools.description: Find the type that defines a variable, parameter, or return value. Use when you have a variable and want to see its interface/class definition, not where the variable itself is declared.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_find_references
tools.description: Find every usage of a symbol across the codebase. Use before renaming or deleting to understand impact. More complete than grep—finds semantic matches, not just text.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

include_declaration.description: Whether to include the declaration in results

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

offset.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination)

tools.name: lsp_find_implementations
tools.description: Find all concrete implementations of an interface, abstract class, or abstract method. Use to understand how an abstraction is actually used in practice.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_hover
tools.description: Get the type signature and documentation for any symbol. Use to understand what a function accepts/returns or what type a variable has, without reading its implementation.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_signature_help
tools.description: Get parameter hints for a function call. Use when you need to know what arguments a function expects and their types, especially for overloaded functions.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_document_symbols
tools.description: Get a structured outline of a file: all functions, classes, interfaces, and variables with their types and relationships. Faster and more accurate than reading and parsing the file yourself.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_workspace_symbols
tools.description: Find any function, class, interface, or type by name—no file path needed. Start here when exploring a codebase or locating where something is defined. Supports fuzzy matching.

query.description: Search query to match symbol names (supports fuzzy matching)

kinds.description: Filter results to specific symbol kinds (e.g., "Class", "Function", "Variable")

tools.name: lsp_file_exports
tools.description: Get the public API surface of a file: top-level functions, classes, interfaces, and variables with their type signatures. Use to understand what a module exposes without reading the entire file.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

include_signatures.description: Include type signatures (slower but more informative)

tools.name: lsp_file_imports
tools.description: Get all imports/dependencies of a file. Shows what modules this file depends on, including ES modules, CommonJS require(), and dynamic imports. Use to understand file dependencies.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_related_files
tools.description: Find files connected to a given file: what it imports and what imports it. Use to understand a file's dependencies and dependents before refactoring.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_diagnostics
tools.description: Get compiler errors, warnings, and hints for a file. Use after making changes to check for type errors, missing imports, or other issues. Results are cached from language server notifications.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_workspace_diagnostics
tools.description: Get all errors and warnings across the entire project—no file path needed. Use during initial analysis to find problems or after changes to check overall project health. Only includes files opened in this session.

limit.description: Maximum number of diagnostics to return

tools.name: lsp_completions
tools.description: Get intelligent code completion suggestions at a position. Returns available methods, properties, variables, and types that are valid in that context. More accurate than text-based completion.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_rename
tools.description: Rename a symbol across the entire codebase safely. Handles all references, imports, and re-exports. Use dry_run=true (default) to preview changes before applying. More reliable than find-and-replace.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

dry_run.description: If true, only preview changes without applying. If false, apply changes to files.

tools.name: lsp_code_actions
tools.description: Get quick fixes and refactoring suggestions at a position. Use to auto-fix errors, organize imports, extract functions, or apply other automated transformations. Set apply=true to execute an action.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

end_line.description: End line number (1-indexed). Defaults to start line.

end_column.description: End column number (1-indexed). Defaults to start column.

kinds.description: Filter by code action kinds: quickfix, refactor, refactor.extract, refactor.inline, refactor.rewrite, source, source.organizeImports, source.fixAll

apply.description: If true, apply the first available action. If false, just list available actions.

action_index.description: Index of the action to apply (when apply=true). Defaults to 0 (first action).

tools.name: lsp_call_hierarchy
tools.description: Trace function calls: who calls this function (incoming) and what it calls (outgoing). Essential for understanding code flow and assessing impact before modifying a function.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

direction.description: Direction of call hierarchy: incoming (callers), outgoing (callees), or both

tools.name: lsp_type_hierarchy
tools.description: Explore inheritance: find parent classes/interfaces (supertypes) and child classes/implementations (subtypes). Use to understand class relationships before modifying a base class or interface.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

direction.description: Direction of type hierarchy: supertypes (parents), subtypes (children), or both

tools.name: lsp_format_document
tools.description: Format a file according to project style settings (prettier, eslint, etc.). Use after making edits to ensure consistent formatting. Set apply=true to write changes.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file to format

insert_spaces.description: Use spaces instead of tabs (default: true)

apply.description: If true, apply formatting changes to file. If false, return edits without applying.

tools.name: lsp_smart_search
tools.description: Get comprehensive information about a symbol in one call: type signature, definition location, all references, implementations, and call hierarchy. Use when you need full context about something instead of making multiple separate queries.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

include.description: Which information to include in results

references_limit.description: Maximum number of references/implementations to return

tools.name: lsp_find_symbol
tools.description: Find a symbol by name alone—no file path or position needed. Returns the definition location plus full context (type info, references, call hierarchy). Use when you know a symbol name but not where it is defined.

name.description: Symbol name to search for (supports fuzzy matching)

include.description: What information to include for the found symbol

references_limit.description: Maximum number of references to include

tools.name: lsp_server_status
tools.description: Check which language servers are running and their state. Use to verify LSP is available before analysis or to debug connection issues.

server_id.description: Specific server ID to check, or omit for all servers

tools.name: lsp_start_server
tools.description: Start a language server manually for a workspace. Usually not needed—servers auto-start when you use other LSP tools. Use only when you need explicit control over server lifecycle.

workspace_root.description: Absolute path to the workspace/project root

tools.name: lsp_stop_server
tools.description: Stop a running language server to free resources. Servers auto-stop after idle timeout, so manual stop is rarely needed.

workspace_root.description: Workspace root to stop server for. If omitted, stops all instances of this server type.

tools.name: lsp_document_highlights
tools.description: Find all occurrences of the symbol at a position within the same file, classified as text/read/write. Faster and more local than lsp_find_references when you only care about one file (e.g., quick local rename preview or read-vs-write analysis).

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_inlay_hints
tools.description: Get inferred type annotations and parameter-name hints for a range of code. Reveals variable types, return types, and named-parameter mappings without one hover per identifier. Ideal for understanding heavily inferred code (TypeScript const/let, Rust let, etc.) at scale.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_selection_range
tools.description: Get the chain of semantic ranges enclosing a position — the expression, then its statement, then its block, up to the file. Use to pick the right range for lsp_code_actions (e.g., "extract this expression vs the whole statement").

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

tools.name: lsp_folding_ranges
tools.description: Get the foldable regions of a file (functions, classes, blocks, imports, comments). Use as a quick structural overview before reading specific sections.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the source file

kind_filter.description: Filter results to one kind of fold

tools.name: lsp_index_files
tools.description: Open a batch of files so language servers begin publishing diagnostics for them. Required warm-up before lsp_workspace_diagnostics (which only sees opened files) and before lsp_related_files imported_by. Use a small targeted set rather than the whole workspace.

files.description: List of absolute file paths to open

findings.title: Scope Creep — 'lsp_goto_definition' accepts write-path param (path traversal risk)
findings.description: Tool 'lsp_goto_definition' accepts a file path parameter (file_path) and its description indicates write operations. Without path sanitization, callers may supply traversal sequences to write files outside the intended directory.
findings.evidence: path param(s): file_path | description: Find where a function, class, or variable is defined. More accurate than grep—handles aliases, re-exports, and overloads correctly. Returns file path, line, and column of the definition.
findings.remediation: Validate and canonicalize file path inputs. Restrict writes to an explicit base directory using path.resolve() or os.path.abspath() checks. Manual verification recommended.

findings.title: Scope Creep — 'lsp_rename' accepts write-path param (path traversal risk)
findings.description: Tool 'lsp_rename' accepts a file path parameter (file_path) and its description indicates write operations. Without path sanitization, callers may supply traversal sequences to write files outside the intended directory.
findings.evidence: path param(s): file_path | description: Rename a symbol across the entire codebase safely. Handles all references, imports, and re-exports. Use dry_run=true (default) to preview changes before applying. More reliable than find-and-replace.
findings.remediation: Validate and canonicalize file path inputs. Restrict writes to an explicit base directory using path.resolve() or os.path.abspath() checks. Manual verification recommended.

findings.description: Tool 'lsp_format_document' accepts a file path parameter (file_path) and its description indicates write operations. Without path sanitization, callers may supply traversal sequences to write files outside the intended directory.
findings.evidence: path param(s): file_path | description: Format a file according to project style settings (prettier, eslint, etc.). Use after making edits to ensure consistent formatting. Set apply=true to write changes.
findings.remediation: Validate and canonicalize file path inputs. Restrict writes to an explicit base directory using path.resolve() or os.path.abspath() checks. Manual verification recommended.

findings.description: Tool 'lsp_document_highlights' accepts a file path parameter (file_path) and its description indicates write operations. Without path sanitization, callers may supply traversal sequences to write files outside the intended directory.
findings.evidence: path param(s): file_path | description: Find all occurrences of the symbol at a position within the same file, classified as text/read/write. Faster and more local than lsp_find_references when you only care about one file (e.g., quick loca
findings.remediation: Validate and canonicalize file path inputs. Restrict writes to an explicit base directory using path.resolve() or os.path.abspath() checks. Manual verification recommended.

findings.title: 'lsp_server_status' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'lsp_code_actions' description reveals arbitrary execution intent
findings.description: Tool 'lsp_code_actions' description contains keywords associated with arbitrary command or shell execution. This may enable sandbox escape or privilege escalation.
findings.evidence: Get quick fixes and refactoring suggestions at a position. Use to auto-fix errors, organize imports, extract functions, or apply other automated transformations. Set apply=true to execute an action.
findings.remediation: Restrict the tool to a well-defined, scoped operation. Do not expose generic shell execution capabilities via MCP tools.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'lsp_find_symbol.name'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: sql).
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'lsp_stop_server.server_id'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: url).
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'lsp_stop_server.workspace_root'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: url).
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: 'lsp_workspace_symbols.query' silently accepts wrong type
findings.description: Expected type 'string', sent str value '' — no error returned.
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'lsp_workspace_symbols' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: 'lsp_server_status' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: 'lsp_stop_server' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: Hidden param accepted — 'lsp_goto_definition' responds differently to _debug
findings.description: Parameter '_debug' caused a response size or format change. The server may be accepting undeclared parameters. Probe response significantly larger: 161 → 270 chars
findings.evidence: Diff: Probe response significantly larger: 161 → 270 chars
findings.remediation: Set additionalProperties: false in the tool schema and reject unknown parameters. Validate all inputs against a strict allowlist.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.
